mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already elevated relative to recent months and CPIAUCSL remains firm, markets infer restrictive policy will persist, lifting expected short rates and pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if 10-year yield changes are negative or flat in most 20-day windows when FEDFUNDS is above its 6-month average and CPIAUCSL is not decelerating; also rejected if the sign accuracy falls below 55% in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a recent baseline weakens growth expectations and boosts duration demand, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall over the next 20 trading days as investors price slower nominal activity.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp versus its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Rejected if yield changes are not negative after UNRATE threshold crossings, or if yield direction is positive in more than 60% of triggered episodes over the evaluation sample.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in RSAFS and HOUST signals resilient household and housing demand, which raises real activity and nominal growth expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if positive yield changes do not cluster when both retail sales and housing starts are improving together, or if the joint signal adds no incremental predictive value over single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in INDPRO while CPIAUCSL remains elevated implies weaker output growth without matching disinflation, which lowers term premium and leads the 10-year Treasury yield to fall over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls while CPIAUCSL stays at or above its 3-month average
failure_condition: Rejected if yield changes are positive in most episodes where industrial production contracts and inflation stays sticky, or if the pattern disappears once the sample is split by inflation regime.

mechanism_narrative: Strong labor-market and spending data, captured by PAYEMS rising alongside RSAFS, imply firmer real demand and stronger nominal growth, which should lift the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if joint PAYEMS-RSAFS strength fails to precede higher yields in a majority of observations, or if the interaction term is not statistically stronger than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5 percent during a tightening cycle, Treasury markets reprice the policy path upward and the 10-year yield falls less than equities over the next 20 trading days because duration becomes more attractive relative to cash only after restrictive policy is already priced in.
failure_condition: Rejected if yield changes are not negative after the threshold is crossed, or if post-threshold episodes show rising yields in more than half of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A low-unemployment regime with PAYEMS still expanding signals persistent labor tightness and wage pressure, which lifts inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE at or below 3.7% and PAYEMS rises over the prior 2 months
failure_condition: Rejected if yields do not trend higher in low-unemployment, payroll-expanding episodes, or if the signal reverses sign when UNRATE is below 3.7%.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, a rebound in GDPC1 combined with rising INDPRO indicates reacceleration in real activity, which raises expected nominal growth and lifts the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if GDP and industrial production rebounds fail to precede higher yields over 60 trading days, or if the interaction loses significance after excluding recession-recovery windows.
